title_original: Breaking ground on India’s quantum future | IBM Quantum Computing Blog
summary_original: India has begun construction on the Quantum Valley Tech Park in Amaravati, set to host the country’s first IBM quantum computer. This initiative coincides with a nationwide push to grow quantum expertise, with a free online quantum computing course by IBM and the Indian Institute of Technology Madras surpassing 208,785 enrollments for 2026, over 100,000 from Andhra Pradesh alone. The tech park, in partnership with Tata Consultancy Services, enables cloud access to IBM’s quantum hardware prior to installation. According to a 2023 report by India’s National Institution for Transforming India, about 100,000 quantum developers will be needed for leadership in the 2030s. This marks a significant step in building India’s quantum workforce and research infrastructure this year.
